title: Bajwara Fort
tagline: Bajwara Fort — heritage city of Punjab

overview.short: Bajwara Fort, also known as Qila Bajwara or Kila Bajwara, is a qila fortress located in Bajwara, Hoshiarpur, Punjab, India that is spread over six to seven acres.
overview.description: Bajwara Fort, also known as Qila Bajwara or Kila Bajwara, is a qila fortress located in Bajwara, Hoshiarpur, Punjab, India that is spread over six to seven acres. The fortress' origin is unknown and it fell into the hands of various rulers. The fortress is endangered due to lack of maintenance and is at risk of total collapse.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Bajwara
topPlaces.description: Bajwara is an old historic town situated in Hoshiarpur District. It is birthplace of Mata Sundri, first wife of Guru Gobind Singh.

topPlaces.name: Ram Colony Camp
topPlaces.description: Ram Colony Camp is a suburb of Hoshiarpur city situated on Chandigarh-Hoshiarpur highway. It is 138 km from state capital Chandigarh.

topPlaces.name: Hoshiarpur
topPlaces.description: Hoshiarpur (Punjabi: [ɦʊʃɪˈaːɾpʊɾ] ) is a city and a municipal corporation in Hoshiarpur district in the Doaba region of the Indian state of Punjab. It was founded, according to tradition, during the early part of the fourteenth century.

topPlaces.name: Boothgarh
topPlaces.description: Boothgarh is a medium-size village located in Khanna Tehsil of Ludhiana district in Punjab, India with a total of 254 families residing. The Boothgarh village has a population of 1190 of which 616 are males while 574 are females as per Population Census 2011.

topPlaces.name: Badial
topPlaces.description: Badial is a small village in the area of Dona in Kapurthala, Punjab, India with a population around 500. It is situated on the road Kala Sanghia-Sidwa Dona.

topPlaces.name: Singriwala
topPlaces.description: Singriwala is a village in Hoshiarpur district, in Punjab, India.

topPlaces.name: 1986 Hoshiarpur bus massacre
topPlaces.description: The 1986 Hoshiarpur Bus Massacre was a massacre of 24 Hindu bus-passengers by Khalistani militants. The bus massacre was the worst terrorist incident in Punjab of the year 1986.

topPlaces.name: Chabbewal
topPlaces.description: Chabbewal is a Village in the district of Hoshiarpur, Punjab, India, with a population of 2698. Chabbewal is situated just in the middle of Hoshiarpur and Mahilpur stretch about 10 km each side on State Highway 24 of the state Punjab that connect Dasuya, Pathankot to Chandigarh.

items.note: Bajwara is an old historic town situated in Hoshiarpur District. It is birthplace of Mata Sundri, first wife of Guru Gobind Singh.
Ram Colony Camp is a suburb of Hoshiarpur city situated on Chandigarh-Hoshiarpur highway. It is 138 km from state capital Chandigarh.

Hoshiarpur (Punjabi: [ɦʊʃɪˈaːɾpʊɾ] ) is a city and a municipal corporation in Hoshiarpur district in the Doaba region of the Indian state of Punjab. It was founded, according to tradition, during the early part of the fourteenth century.

Boothgarh is a medium-size village located in Khanna Tehsil of Ludhiana district in Punjab, India with a total of 254 families residing. The Boothgarh village has a population of 1190 of which 616 are males while 574 are females as per Population Census 2011.
Badial is a small village in the area of Dona in Kapurthala, Punjab, India with a population around 500. It is situated on the road Kala Sanghia-Sidwa Dona.
Singriwala is a village in Hoshiarpur district, in Punjab, India.
The 1986 Hoshiarpur Bus Massacre was a massacre of 24 Hindu bus-passengers by Khalistani militants. The bus massacre was the worst terrorist incident in Punjab of the year 1986.

Chabbewal is a Village in the district of Hoshiarpur, Punjab, India, with a population of 2698. Chabbewal is situated just in the middle of Hoshiarpur and Mahilpur stretch about 10 km each side on State Highway 24 of the state Punjab that connect Dasuya, Pathankot to Chandigarh.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bajwara Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bajwara Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bajwara Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (78 km) and the nearest railway station is Ludhiana Junction (68 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bajwara Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bajwara Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bajwara Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Bajwara Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Bajwara Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bajwara, Ram Colony Camp, Hoshiarpur, Boothgarh, Badial.
